title: Diabetes follow-up

red_flags.text: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth type 2 diabetes scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped adult type 2 diabetes telehealth presentation fields with scope limitations retained.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth type 2 diabetes scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

red_flags.text: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped foot-ulcer, visual, neuropathy, and clinician-referral context as prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

investigations.text: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped home glucose, hypoglycaemia, lifestyle, BP, BMI, foot, renal, ACR, HbA1c, lipid, podiatry, retinal, and annual-review fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

investigations.text: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed HbA1c, renal, lipid, and urine ACR documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

exact_section.heading: Appendix 7 — summary of required laboratory tests

medication.text: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped adherence, glucose-monitoring, and clinician-decided medication-plan documentation without dose or autonomous change.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.

source_limitations: Type 1 diabetes, children, pregnancy, and non-telehealth settings are not fully covered by the exact source.

General acute-infection negatives, chest pain or breathlessness, detailed pulse or sensation findings, injection sites, cardiac examination, sick-day rules, rising or target-based follow-up labels, fixed follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and generic clinician plan remain unsupported.
Diabetes type, control, complications, result interpretation, medication choice or dose, treatment change, referral urgency, and follow-up interval remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
